stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and reduced memory usage for models.
The updated engine also contributes to higher quality outputs from the language models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Thread Direct looks to solve Matter’s biggest setup headache
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Thread Direct is a new feature for the Thread smart home networking protocol.
It allows users to onboard Thread devices without needing a dedicated Thread border router.
Setup can now be accomplished using only a mobile device equipped with a Thread radio.

stories.title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
stories.author: Hao Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EvolveNav, a framework for Zero-Shot Object-Goal Navigation (ZS-OGN) that allows agents to learn without prior training.
Addresses the limitation of existing foundation model methods by incorporating a self-evolving memory for continuous test-time improvement.
The core innovation is building an 'agentic rule memory' by extracting actionable knowledge from the environment.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel looped architecture for world modeling.
Addresses the trade-off between faithful, long-horizon simulation and computational expense in current models.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency by iteratively refining latent states using a parameter-shared transformer block.

stories.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
stories.author: Nicola Franco
bullet_points: The study performs a red-team adversarial robustness evaluation on Anthropic's frontier LLMs (Fable 5 and Opus 4.8).
The evaluation utilized the HackAgent framework against four families of automated jailbreak attacks across 7,826 harmful intents.
The findings indicate that both models demonstrated significant resistance against the majority of the adversarial attempts.

stories.title: The Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset: Reconstructing U.S. Corporate and Financial Disclosures into Layout-Faithful and Token-Efficient Pretraining Data
stories.author: Kay Giesecke
bullet_points: Introduces the Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset (SEFD), an open-source reconstruction of SEC filings.
Addresses the scarcity and cost of high-quality, long-context training data for Large Language Models (LLMs).
Formats the data into layout-faithful MultiMarkdown specifically for training financial language models.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel looped architecture for world modeling.
Addresses the tension between faithful long-horizon simulation and computational cost in current world models.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency by iteratively refining latent states using a parameter-shared transformer block.

stories.title: Looped World Models
stories.author: Wai Lam
bullet_points: Introduces Looped World Models (LoopWM), a novel looped architecture for world modeling.
Addresses the tension between faithful long-horizon simulation and computational cost in current world models.
Achieves up to 100x parameter efficiency by iteratively refining latent states using a parameter-shared transformer block.

stories.title: A Red-Team Study of Anthropic Fable 5 & Opus 4.8 Models
stories.author: Nicola Franco
bullet_points: The study performs a red-teaming analysis on Anthropic's frontier LLMs (Fable 5 and Opus 4.8) to test their adversarial robustness.
It evaluates resistance against four families of automated jailbreak attacks across 7,826 harmful intents.
The methodology uses the HackAgent framework and employs a panel of three judge models for independent adjudication of attack successes.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models without cloud dependency.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has temporarily halted the implementation of token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK.
The planned billing change was anticipated to significantly raise costs for heavy users.
This pause suggests a response to user feedback or a strategic adjustment in pricing models for AI agents.

stories.title: Pentagon boasts of using AI to write reports mandated by Congress
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The Pentagon is reportedly utilizing AI to generate reports required by Congress.
The Department of Defense claims that 1.5 million personnel are currently using generative AI tools.
This highlights the increasing integration and adoption of generative AI within major government defense agencies.

stories.title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
stories.author: Dhruv Shah
bullet_points: Introduces VERITAS, a generator-verifier framework for improving generalist robot policies.
The framework uses a pre-trained policy as a 'generator' and a gradient-free 'visual verifier' for real-time evaluation.
Enables inference-time policy steering and autonomous self-improvement for robots operating in the real world.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale the evaluation of research reproducibility.
Addresses the major bottleneck in current reproducibility benchmarks: the high manual effort required for data curation.
Leverages naturally occurring human-raised GitHub issues as a scalable form of supervision for testing reproduction capabilities.

stories.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
stories.author: Xenofon Koutsoukos
bullet_points: The research focuses on developing autonomous cyber-defense agents using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to combat sophisticated cyber-attacks.
The agents utilize neurosymbolic approaches, specifically behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs), to enable reasoning and adaptation.
A key challenge addressed is the partial observability of the system, where the attacker's (red agent's) actions are not fully known.

stories.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
stories.author: Ahmed A. Metwally
bullet_points: Introduces RubricsTree, a new framework to address the open-ended evaluation bottleneck for personal health agents.
Addresses the limitations of current evaluation methods: physician annotation is costly, while LLM-as-a-judge methods are subjective.
Focuses on scaling the evaluation of LLM-empowered health agents that incorporate user health metrics for better healthcare access.

stories.title: DRFLOW: A Deep Research Benchmark for Personalized Workflow Prediction
stories.author: Issam H. Laradji
bullet_points: Introduces DRFLOW, a new deep research benchmark designed for personalized workflow prediction.
Addresses a gap in current deep research systems, which often focus on summarizing reports rather than identifying concrete, sequential action-step workflows.
Highlights the practical need for AI agents to map out complex, multi-step enterprise processes (e.g., 'How do I request new headcount?').

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale reproducibility audits in machine learning research.
Addresses the scalability bottleneck in current reproducibility benchmarks, which rely heavily on manual data curation.
Leverages human-raised GitHub issues as a method for natural, realistic supervision during reproduction evaluation.

stories.title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
stories.author: Xenofon Koutsoukos
bullet_points: The research proposes using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to train autonomous cyber-defense agents against sophisticated attacks.
These agents utilize neurosymbolic approaches, specifically behavior trees with learning-enabled components (LECs), to enable reasoning and adaptation.
The core challenge addressed is handling the partially observable nature of the cyber-attack environment (the 'red agent's' actions).

stories.title: Multi-Source Cybersecurity Logs: An ATT&CK-Labeled Dataset and SLM Evaluation
stories.author: Md Rayhanur Rahman
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of detecting multi-stage cyberattacks by requiring correlation across system, network, and browser logs.
A new dataset, ATLAS, is introduced to provide labeled, multi-source data necessary for training machine learning models for cybersecurity.
Existing public datasets are insufficient because they typically lack telemetry from one or more critical sources (host, network, or browser).

stories.title: Kolmogorov Regression for Robust Diffusion Policies
stories.author: Lekan Molu
bullet_points: Addresses temporal drift in finite-dimensional diffusion policies, a problem affecting long-horizon performance in physical systems.
Introduces a backward Kolmogorov equation to lift diffusion policies into a Cameron-Martin space.
Replaces stochastic score matching with a deterministic boundary-value Partial Differential Equation (PDE) problem.

stories.title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
stories.author: Ameet Talwalkar
bullet_points: Introduces ReproRepo, a new framework designed to scale reproducibility audits in AI research.
Addresses the scalability issue in current reproducibility benchmarks, which rely heavily on manual data curation.
Leverages naturally occurring human-raised GitHub issues as a source of supervision for realistic reproduction testing.

stories.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
stories.author: Ahmed A. Metwally
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of open-ended evaluation for LLM-powered personal health agents.
Proposes RubricsTree, a scalable framework to overcome the limitations of costly physician annotation and subjective LLM-as-a-judge methods.
Focuses on improving the reliability and scalability of assessing health agents across health memory and medical skills.

stories.title: Learning from the Self-future: On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs
stories.author: Shiwei Liu
bullet_points: Introduces d-OPSD, the first On-policy Self-distillation (OPSD) framework specifically designed for diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs).
Addresses a key limitation in existing OPSD methods, which are inherently autoregressive and conflict with the arbitrary-order generation of dLLMs.
Focuses on adapting post-training distillation techniques to the unique architectural challenges presented by diffusion-based LLMs.

stories.title: The Measurement Gap in the Automation of EU Law: Benchmarking Doctrinal Legal Reasoning under the EU AI Act
stories.author: Michèle Finck
bullet_points: Current LLMs can generate legal text of median quality, but existing benchmarks fail to test core doctrinal legal reasoning.
The research highlights a 'measurement gap' between current AI evaluations and the complex interpretive work required in law.
This gap is critical because the EU AI Act mandates 'appropriate accuracy' for high-risk AI in the judicial domain.

stories.title: Show HN: High-Res Neural Cellular Automata
stories.author: esychology
bullet_points: Researchers have advanced Neural Cellular Automata (CAs) to generate high-definition (HD) patterns in real-time.
The key innovation involves transforming each CA cell into a Neural Field, enabling higher resolution and dynamic behavior.
Demos showcase applications such as pattern growth/healing, synthesizing regenerative PBR textures, and creating 3D textures.

stories.title: A 27-Year-Old Authentication Bypass in OpenBSD's PPP Stack
stories.author: blog.argus-systems.ai via fro
bullet_points: A significant, long-standing security vulnerability (27 years) was found in OpenBSD's PPP stack.
The vulnerability is an authentication bypass, posing a serious risk to systems using the affected software.
This highlights the importance of long-term security maintenance and vulnerability patching in established operating systems.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: roman.pt via hoistbypetard
bullet_points: The story concerns a security vulnerability or 'backdoor' discovered within a LinkedIn job offer context.
This suggests potential risks related to phishing, social engineering, or platform security on LinkedIn.
The topic is relevant to cybersecurity and professional networking platforms, making it a moderate but interesting tech discussion.

stories.title: Spring AI: The Senior Dev's Honest Take on Java's AI Moment
stories.author: sayed_ali_alkamel
bullet_points: Spring AI provides portable abstractions to integrate enterprise Java applications with various AI models.
Key features highlighted include support for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Multi-Agent Communication Protocols (MCP), and tool calling.
The article offers a practical guide on when developers should utilize Spring AI in their projects.

stories.title: Your AI Provider Is a Single Point of Failure
stories.author: maishsk
bullet_points: The article discusses a regulatory issue concerning AI providers.
The U.S. Commerce Department issued a letter to Anthropic.
The topic highlights the risk of relying on a single AI provider (Single Point of Failure).
